tribe_description: This micro-cluster consists of tech-savvy gaming enthusiasts who test hardware and software in depth, then translate those findings into community-oriented advice. Their writing style mirrors their characteristics: reviews are often specification-rich, comparison-driven, and peppered with troubleshooting guidance or price-to-performance judgments.
tribe_definition: A tribe of performance-value gamers who judge products through a gaming and entertainment lens. They generally prioritize real-world performance, value for money.
population_definition: Gamers who evaluate video games, consoles, accessories, and entertainment products.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: Hands-on testers who benchmark and compare products
Value-conscious but willing to pay for true quality
Detail-rich communicators including setup or fix instructions
Quick to call out flaws and praise innovations

main.text: Ensuring hassle-free installation and everyday use

validation_triggers: Strong technical performance or clear audio/visual quality
Fair or bargain pricing for the feature set provided
Comfortable, ergonomic designs that improve long sessions
Customizable controls, RGB, or modular components
Cable-free convenience and solid build materials
friction_points: Software bugs, setup hurdles, or poor compatibility
Overpriced items or intrusive monetization schemes
Flimsy construction or short component life
Limited game/content libraries or missing features
Lag, latency, or other performance shortfalls

implicit_goals.text: Build or maintain an optimal, trouble-free gaming setup without overspending
Influence both peers and manufacturers through evidence-based feedback
Continually upgrade gear to keep pace with advancing performance standards

Playing on PC.

It took me forever to be able to install and play this game. I first had to update to anniversary edition of windows 10. It took me having to contact Microsoft to get that installed on my computer because it wouldn't install correctly. Then I was finally able to install the game. After 47.9GB download of the game I couldn't play it still because it was saying my graphics card drivers were not up to date. AMD R9 390. Used AMD auto detect and it said everything was up to date. Well there is Crimson Edition that you need to install to play this game. It's not the 21. version that the game says you need its a 16.8 I believe on AMDs website under drivers. Well after that I have been able to play it for a while, but now that I have finally made it through a few venue setups on the game it now crashes after a few minutes playing. Trying to find out whats wrong with that now.

Now its not like I'm running a weak setup. I have an i7-4790k with an R9 390 and 16GB ram so it should play anything even though this game is a little more ram dependent that others. Usually 8gb ram is plenty for video gaming but not for this one, I think it requires 12gb, weird number.

Anyways there is my rant. Hope this helps.

AHZGQXTGR3WB6CQR3PP2TB2YPTUA_1.product_description: KOFIRE UG-06 Wireless Gaming Headset for PS4 PS5 PC, 48H Playtime, Low Latency, Noise Cancelling Over Ear Bluetooth Gaming Headphones with Dual Mic, 3D Surround Sound, RGB Lighting
AHZGQXTGR3WB6CQR3PP2TB2YPTUA_1.review_text:
This headset comes with Bluetooth 5.0 version, which is the latest Bluetooth technology at this time. The connection was exceptionally steady, and lag-free audio of low latency within 40ms, which is relatively low compared to other wireless headphones in the price range.
Being a fast-paced gaming enthusiast, you must know how a high latency can ruin your gameplay. Therefore, I have always preferred wired headphones due to their latency, but the UG-06 completely changed my perception of wireless headphones.
I often get nervous while playing using a wired gaming headset due to the cable. It rolls around the chair, and as an aggressive gamer (not proud of) with uncontrollable hand movements, I often end up pulling the cord out of the 3.5mm jack. With the UG-06 headphone, playing games is a breeze, and I have more room for hand movements.
The UG-06 comes with beautiful RGB light, which is one of its major attractions besides its sleek black body. I don’t use the RGB lighting much, so I often turn them off while playing. I only turn them for 2-3 hours at night time. Right now, the headphone gives 30 hours of playtime at a single charge. The good thing is that even at a low charge, I didn’t find any compromise in sound quality, which is often a problem in many wireless headsets.
Even if your charge goes off, you can connect the 3.5mm audio cable to the headset and use it as a wired headset. The only problem is that you won’t be able to use the RGB lights and mute function while using the wired connection. Even with RGB lights off, visual appearance is really cool. They have this shiny hematite looking detail covering the ear cups (kinda like black mirror) giving it cool and futuristic look. You can see on the pictures and the video I am also uploading. Furthemore, cups are padded with super soft padding making them really comfortable. Headband is also nicely padded and adjustable, it doesn’t squeeze my brains out after longer use.
Coming to quality, I am in awe of its 3D immersive sound effects. I played Horizon Forbidden West, and I cannot praise it enough for its 50 mm super-size audio driver that provides a broader sound field and an ultra-immersive gaming experience.
Pros
• Steady Connection & Low Latency for lag-free sound in fast-paced games
• Adjustable headband and soft fully-enclosing earmuff for superior grip and all-day comfort
• 30 hours of continuous playtime
• Dual microphone with noise canceling feature
• Crystal-clear calls with HD voice during games
• 7 Colorful RGB Light on both sides with automatic color changing loop

AHJYTEUCAGPLF4ESBSA64AZARH3A.product_description: Go behind enemy lines with the ultimate modern military shooter. Choose your missions across an unforgiving open world. Explore large open world maps with dynamic weather and a day/night cycle that actually impacts your play and decisions. Sniper Ghost Warrior 3 is the story of brotherhood, faith and betrayal in a land soaked in the blood of civil war.
AHJYTEUCAGPLF4ESBSA64AZARH3A.review_text:
Let me state right up front! I am playing this game on a standard PS4, and it does state that the game is PS4 Pro enhanced.

The game looks HORRIBLE! It's the worst looking game I have played on a PS4...It's a joke! Horrible graphics, poor modeling bad animations. Everything about its appearance is just plain bad! If it was JUST the graphics, I could overlook that! I mean, I still play and enjoy PS3 and PS2 games! How does it get worse? Well, in terms of story, it has the most uninspiring and just outright ridiculous plot I have ever seen in a game. EVER!

Then the game is insanely laggy! Often times, especially when driving the game will freeze, jump and jerk! It's impossible to stay on the road, because of the freeze frame moments! One minute I'm cruising down a road, then the next I'm smashed right into a tree because the game froze for a minute!

Then there's the AI! The AI is incredibly difficult, or just outright glitched! They can see through obstacles and even shoot through them on occasion! Then there's the fact that you might as well not even use your suppressor. Even with it on, one shot and every enemy in the map zeros in on you and comes charging...it's just stupid!

Overall. the game seems like a buggy budget game that should NOT have been released yet! Save your money and buy Ghost Recon Wildlands, or Sniper Elite 4! This game is just a waste of your money!

AHJYTEUCAGPLF4ESBSA64AZARH3A.product_description: Redefine your expectations of immersion in gaming with moments so intense your intuition takes over. Step into incredible virtual worlds and overcome new challenges in extraordinary ways.
AHJYTEUCAGPLF4ESBSA64AZARH3A.review_text:
The VR headset works incredibly well! Granted, I'm not a fan of the camera or the move controllers...thankfully not all games require you to use the move controllers. The headset is very lightweight, though did make my eyes and brow sweaty...to be fair, I live in a desert where the heat is already 100+ degrees. The headset provides a huge viewing area, and the rubber flaps on the sides blocks out all outside light. This adds to the immersion of the games!

The headset is a perfect fit, even with my giant noggin, and even if you wear glasses you can still use the headset since the eyepiece can be adjusted a bit as well.

My only complaint is the USB ports required to connect everything up. A PS4 has 2 USB ports. For me, one is being used for my gamepad, and the other is being used for extended storage. This complicates things with the PSVR since the headset connects to your PS4 with a USB cord, and your 2 move controllers need to connect via USB to charge. That's 3 needed USB ports, on a system with only 2 USB ports! The move controllers can't even be hooked upo and charge through your PC....it has to be through your console. Now, you would THINK sony would include something to fix this...a USB splitter or something in a launch bundle...but nope! It's really annoying...

Other than that? Great VR unit! Just wish their were more games!

AHNM5TRS66Y25IPXQK432ERWCEPA.product_description: DRAGON QUEST BUILDERS 2 - Nintendo Switch [Digital Code]
AHNM5TRS66Y25IPXQK432ERWCEPA.review_text:
I played and loved the origianl DQB, and as well as being an overall fan of Dragon Quest in general, so this was a must-buy for me.

The game is extremely engaging. No spoilers here, but I'm in the third chapter now. Now sure how big overall the game is. There are tons of improvements to the original which makes it a worthwhile investment. Even if you've never played Dragon Quest Builders 1, or the mainline franchise, if you like crafting and building, this is a great game.

I dock one star for a few issues:

* Slow pacing. Like a traditional JRPG, this has TONS of dialog. Most of the dialog is easy to step thru by just mashing the A button, but there are some story-centric scenes which cannot be skipped and just take forever to wait out.
* Very small UI scale. In handheld mode, it can make it a squint-fest and there is no way to increase the font or stuff.
* I haven't unlocked free-play mode. I guess just like the original, you have to finish the game first. That might be a turnoff to some people who just want to be creative. There is a built-in social media network where you can share screenies and some of those are AMAZING, I just wish we could get there sooner.
* Frame rating issues. Generally the game runs smoothly, but I've had one boss battle which was fairly involved, NPCs running all over the place, monsters popping up, and the boss itself hurling attacks, and it really hammered the Switch's performance.

Issues aside, it's a great game and no regrets.

Who else named their slime Rimuru?

AHNM5TRS66Y25IPXQK432ERWCEPA.product_description: My Time at Portia - Nintendo Switch [Digital Code]
AHNM5TRS66Y25IPXQK432ERWCEPA.review_text:
Edit May 19. Shortly after my original review, they released the waited-for patch, but it doesn't garner an upgraded rating. See the end for additional patch comments.

TL;DR - as much fun as this game is, it is equal part frustration. Wait and see if the upcoming patches address the performance but until then, I can't in good heart recommend this game.

Possible spoilers ahead.

I've been playing this since the eshop release date and have finished the main story, which currently ends after the final battle (and one followup mission to reconstruct). Apparently the PC versions are at a higher release than the console versions, and as of this writing, the developers promise a patch "sometime" in May, to add additional content as well as addressing deficiencies in the console ports.

If you like Story of Seasons or Stardew Valley type games, you'll feel at home here. Rather than farming, this game focuses on crafting. You're set in a post-apocalyptic world. You are a Builder whose job is to use remnants of lost technology to craft things to the behest of the mayor or the other townspeople. Like SoS or STV, there is a whole social order where you befriend people and can marry.

There is plenty to do and see. The main story line leads you to improving your technology, creating newer and better machines. You'll unlock new zones with higher level resources to collect. Most of the new zones have type types of ruins for you to explore; the abandoned ruins are where you mine for ore and collect forgotten technology. The hazardous ruins are where you combat monsters and have a better chances of collecting forgotten technologies. You'll need those, as some of the items are un-craftable.

There are also secondary missions, usually unrelated to the main story but sometimes unlocked after certain events. Then there are also commissions which is typically in the form of an NPC asking for something to be crafted.

There is a bit of combat but it's kinda lame with limited options. You basically just mash the attack button. During some of the main missions, you'll be accompanied by NPCs who are stronger than you and can even do most of the combat for you. During the leadup to the final battle, including the final battle itself, I basically sat on the perimeter and let the NPCs beat the bosses.

There are a lot of technical issues with the game which prevent full enjoyment.

The initial loading screen is unbearably long. We're talking upwards of 5 MINUTES. There appears to be serious performance issues with the game where you will experience this enormous lag in various places. Zoning in and out takes a noticeable amount of time. Navigating menus can be exceedingly slow. There is one series of secondary missions which asks you to take a series of photographs, and if you make a mistake, it take take an inappropriately long time to move thru the menus to take photographs. The game does not use the built-in Switch screenshot mechanism for the in-game photos.

At least twice, when zoning out of a building, I fell thru the ground, and just kept plummeting forever. The game automatically puts you to sleep after a specific in-game time, so I just had to wait around for that to happen. Luckily there is no penalty for staying up too late.

Three times the game simply crashed, and the switch reported something to the effect that "the software has encountered an error and will close".

During the final battles, your Switch will slow down to a crawl where controller presses may feel as if they were not registered.

There are other issues (such as terrible proof-reading and grammar usage), but none as bad as the performance issues. After a game crash, if it didn't take 5 minutes to load, I would be a little less annoyed. And while falling thru the sky, the same loading time prevents me from simply re-starting the the Switch home screen. I also like to play different games, but I really have to stop and think "if I stop this to play Diablo for an hour or so, I'll have to wait forever for it to load".

Speaking of Diablo, I cannot accept the smug statement that the switch is "underpowered", when games like Diabo, and other games like first-party Nintendo stuff, can manage better. Even "simpler" games like Farm Together perform better, which said game has noted issues with distance and mild lag during auto-saves, but can still handle counting thousands of individual timers for each crop planted without performance hits.

Path 1.0.4 notes.

With the 1.0.4 patch, they've tried to address some of the game-stopping issues. Load times are still abysmal. Performance hits seem more common now. Graphics lag has increased. When exiting menus, there is a noticeable delay before the game response again. Crashes are more abundant - in the time I've been playing the patch (4 days?), I've crashed five times.

On a positive note :-/ They've added much more content. The main story continues after reconstruction, and there is a new area (as well as new ruins). Without having played the PC versions, it appears the Switch version is now more in-line with that.

AGXCV25HDW4JXI5QQSJTIZUHPRHQ.review_text:
I originally played the PS4 version almost all the way through then found myself in a situation with only a PS3 and wanted to play this game again.

Visually it's only a slight downgrade from the PS4 version, and you have no first-person on-foot camera. Otherwise it's the same game with a few subtle differences (that may or may not be absolutely real, but from my experience they seem pretty clear).

My only real gripe is that the PS3 version suffers from the old GTA issue of generic car types. You will see instances where every single car is of one style until you drive to a different part of the map. This doesn't happen to this extent in the PS4 version.

Two things that I like better: car damage seems more specific on the PS3. If a car drives into you from a front angle you very well may lose your front tire, either completely or it may just be deformed to the point of not spinning. I don't remember this happening as much on the PS4. In general the PS3 vehicles aren't as hearty, which in many ways makes the game more fun, IMO.

Next, the stock market. It feels like the stock market is rigged on the PS4. I've had all three characters invested in one slumped stock for WEEKS without it ever rising. That just isn't realistic in the GTA game world. I think there must be some Rockstar skullduggery there to "punish" players, essentially, and to keep them from earning too much money. On the PS3 you may find instances where a stock with linger at a low rate for a while, but it will always recover. This makes it easier, I guess, to make money. But I don't see keeping my characters poor as any kind of positive in the gaming world.

By continually investing and doing the first couple of Lester assassination missions I've got all my players with huge amounts of money (always invested, unless I'm buying guns & ammo or modding my stock vehicles - which is totally worth the money).

But those two things - car damage and the stock market - make the PS3 version superior in my book.

Oh...*stock market glitch*...all of my characters have well over $100 million each at this point. As the numbers get higher and you try to invest in a new stock sometimes the transaction won't work. You ask it to buy as many shares as your money will buy and it just won't take your money. If this happens to you, split your stock purchases up into several smaller transactions. It's a pain, but it's the only workaround I've found.

AEIUSWJRF3KT7ETGCV5AG6DCSL4Q.product_description: Developed by Spiders Studio for PlayStation 3, PlayStation 4, and Xbox 360, the RPG Bound by Flame drags you into a heroic and desperate struggle, in which the Alliance eventually yielded in front of the inexorable advance of Dead army. From their castles of ice, the lord-sorcerers of Shadow cold now reign as masters on the continent and crush mercilessly the remnant resistance, of which you belong. When all hope appears to be lost, a demon of flames chooses you as its host... Create and customize a powerful warrior, develop your skills (combat, assassination and fire magic) and call upon the devastating powers of the demon within. Find, create and improve your weapons and armor, and find help from numerous companions to help you fight Shadow dragons, Leeches and the fearsome warriors of Dead army in perilous and spectacular real-time battles! AEIUSWJRF3KT7ETGCV5AG6DCSL4Q.review_text:
The graphics are very late PS3 era. Nothing on here will make you want to show off the game to others...it is not a pretty game, it doesn't make you go "ooh" or "ahh" so I would recommend to go the cheaper route and get it for the PS3 unless you just want to add something to your empty PS4 library like me.

The story is pretty cool but the characters back stories and personalities are not that entirely interesting and I found myself sticking to one companion (a NPC who fights alongside you) because she was the only that healed me consistently throughout the game. The game is one of those RPG's that allow for your choices to affect the story but I was deeply disappointed in just how linear or how little your choices actually effected the narrative. If you are thinking that this game is as deep as Mass Effect, KOTOR, or even as diverse as the underrated Alpha Protocol...you will be sadly disappointed and it will negatively effect your desire to replay the game. There is a lot of instances where a character will ask you to choose only to find out that no matter what you choose the outcome will sadly be the same just maybe a sentence or two different.
------------
For example:

Main character: "You will die!"
Villain: "Spare me, spare me"

-Your choices. Kill him or Spare him-

1. Outcome if you Kill him: Main character taunts him and then kills the villain.
2. Outcome if you spare the villain: The villain tries to attack you and you kill him.

Either way, he dies and nobody in the story cares how nor does it effect anything thereafter.
---------------------------

So it was quite a bit of a bummer to find out that 90% of your choices lead to the same immediate outcome.

The fighting mechanics are easy enough if you are fighting one-on-one but you have to rely on setting traps and luring enemies to get hurt or die. You really can't just go in and start hacking and slashing away at the enemies which is a good thing. On the default difficulty I made the mistake thinking this game was a typical modern action rpg where very little planning and careful timing is required. This game is a lot like Demon Souls and Witcher 2. A nice blend of the two. To be honest, I did have fun playing this game and I was thoroughly engaged the whole time while playing this budget title and I do see myself playing this again real soon. I do have to warn you about the hard final boss in this game. Cheap. Very cheap. Just save a ton of traps and either shoot him with arrows or keep blasting him with fire as you lure him into your deathtraps. I made a serious mistake in not coming into the fight well-equipped with traps. Major mistake. It is a serious, serious pain in the butt and tedious to attack him head on. Trust me when I say --- traps are your best friend in this game.

I did not find the voice acting to be terrible like others claim. Sure it's not the best by any means but I've heard worst and I was never cringing but of course I've heard that the main female voice actor is thee worst voice ever but I only played as a male and he was pretty good.

This is indeed budget title but I had fun playing it and as far as I can there are three different endings depending on your final choice in the game.

AGDY4OKMUR4LUK2AD3MHMJNNNCMQ.review_text: Would give a 3.5/5 ideally. These are a sufficient product that do what they're supposed to but aren't the best thing on the market for it. Really only the ones with the little grippers are worth using, the more attractive and smooth ones might as well be thrown away. These go over the existing stock grips. and they would have more stability if you were meant to take that part off before putting these on, and I don't think I can argue much of a case for them. given that. If you  find a pad with actual stick extenders. those will be more stable as long as you screw them on probably too tight. Given that these AceShot grips are kinda expensive. that's what I'd suggest. is to fully upgrade your controller to something with stick extenders. I have the Hori FPS 360 pad I'm using for PC but IDK if there's anyrthing comparable for xbox1 specifically. If you're only buying these for the short grips, then yeah, the short one with the nubby grips is pretty great. The short ones kinda outperform the tall ones in this case, to be honest.
